tokenBar.baseHint: safety rules, skills, messaging, reply format
tokenBar.toolingHint: tool definitions, CLI reference, call style
tokenBar.workspaceHint: injected files — AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, USER.md, etc.
tokenBar.estimateNote: Token counts estimated at ~4 chars per token.

retention.pruneNote: OpenClaw prunes session data after 30 days by default.
retention.resetArchiveDesc: how long .reset files are kept (defaults to pruneAfter)
retention.filesNotDeleted: Session files are not deleted immediately.
retention.deletedDesc: when a session is archived. The file is renamed from
retention.resetDesc: when context is compacted mid-session. The old transcript is renamed to
retention.bothSuffixed: Both suffixed files still count toward token usage. They are only physically deleted after
retention.askOpenClaw: Or ask OpenClaw with the above keywords and let it change it for you.

overview.noModelData: No model activity yet. Start a coding session to see model usage here.
overview.excludesCron: Excludes cron jobs and cron-related sessions
overview.noSessionsFound: No sessions recorded today. Activity will appear here once an agent starts.
overview.noTimelineData: No activity data for this period.

agents.activeDesc:
Active — includes running and stuck agents.
Session file updated within the last 30 min.
agents.idleDesc:
Idle — no session file updates for 30+ min.
Agent has run before but is not currently active.
agents.stillDesc:
Still — agent has never run or has no session data.
No activity has been recorded for this agent.
agents.errorAgentsDesc: Agents whose last session ended with an error or have recent consecutive failures.
agents.healthyDesc: No tool failures, no stop errors, and context below 80% in the last 10 messages.
agents.warningDesc: 1–2 tool failures in the last 10 messages; OR hit output length limit; OR context at 80–99%.
agents.errorDesc: ≥ 3 tool failures; OR stop_reason = error; OR context at 100% (limit reached).
agents.healthScanNote: Scans the last 10 messages of the most recent non-cron session per agent. Auto-resets to Healthy if the last message is older than 6 hours.

sessions.dataSourceBody: Data is read from JSONL files on disk:
sessions.resetExplain: — OpenClaw archived this session when the context was reset (e.g. new conversation started, or context pressure was too high). The agent's long-term memory is unaffected; only the in-context conversation history is cleared.
sessions.deletedExplain: — Session was explicitly deleted in OpenClaw. Still shown here for full history.
sessions.cacheHitTooltip:
% of input tokens served from cache.
cache hit rate = cache_read / (cache_read + input_tokens)
sessions.contextPressureTooltip:
How close the session is to its model's context window limit.
context used = last message's input + cache_read + cache_write
limit = model's max context window (e.g. claude = 1M, gpt-5.2-codex = 272K)
sessions.burnRateTooltip:
Measures context window growth speed.
data: last 3 messages' context size
(input + cache_read + cache_write)
sessions.lastSignalTooltip: The signal from the last assistant message's stop_reason. This does NOT mean the session is finished — more messages may follow.
sessions.contextWindowPerStep: context window size per step · limit: {{limit}}
sessions.avgTooltip:
Average tokens per assistant message.

total tokens / message count

High values indicate large context windows (long conversations or big system prompts). Useful for identifying sessions that may benefit from compaction.

settings.reingestDesc: Re-ingest performs a full wipe of all session data and rebuilds the database from scratch using only the session files currently on disk.
settings.reingestWarning: All existing session data, messages, tool calls, and audit events will be deleted first, then re-imported from the current files on disk. Sessions whose files have been pruned by OpenClaw will not be recoverable.
settings.reingestStep1: Delete all existing sessions, messages, tool calls, and audit events
settings.reingestStep2: Re-parse every session file currently on disk and import from scratch
settings.reingestStep3: Re-run all audit analysis (including any custom rules you have added)
settings.reingestStep4: Rebuild baselines and recalculate risk scores
settings.rulesSafe: Your custom audit rules are safe — they are stored separately and will not be deleted.
settings.reingestFailed: Re-ingest failed. Check that the server is running.
settings.reingestSuccess: Re-ingestion complete. {{files}} files processed, {{sessions}} sessions updated.

claw.title: 🦞 Adopt a Claw
claw.desc: A little creature that wanders along the bottom of the screen. Click it to switch its mood:

audit.title: Audit
audit.subtitle: Agent activity log · file access · external calls · sensitive data
audit.label: Label
audit.noCustomPatterns: No custom data patterns yet. Built-in patterns detect 30+ common secret formats.
audit.noCustomRules: No custom rules yet. Built-in defaults cover SSH, .env, Keychains, and common credential patterns.

injectionPatterns.roleHijackDesc: "You are now / act as / pretend to be"

anomalySignals.unusualHoursDesc: Activity outside the agent's typical active hours (learned from baseline)
anomalySignals.unusualVolumeDesc: Session tool call count exceeds 3x the agent's historical average
anomalySignals.unusualPathDesc: File access in directories the agent has never visited in baseline period
anomalySignals.newDomainDesc: HTTP request to a domain never seen in the agent's history

audit.eventTypeTooltipIntro: Event types are derived from the agent's tool calls. Only events with at least one risk flag appear in the audit timeline.

eventTypes.fileReadDesc: Agent read a file (Read, Image tools). Flagged when reading sensitive paths (.ssh, .env, keychains) or when output contains secrets.
eventTypes.fileWriteDesc: Agent wrote or edited a file (Write, Edit tools). Flagged when targeting sensitive paths or system config files.
eventTypes.execDesc: Agent executed a shell command (Bash, Exec tools). Flagged for destructive commands (rm -rf, chmod 777), elevated commands (sudo, ssh, curl), or data exfiltration patterns.
eventTypes.webFetchDesc: Agent made an HTTP request (WebFetch tool). Flagged when accessing domains not seen in the agent's baseline history.
eventTypes.webSearchDesc: Agent performed a web search (WebSearch tool). Flagged when the search domain is unknown to the agent's baseline.

riskTooltip.highDesc: Immediate action required. Damage has occurred or is in progress.
riskTooltip.mediumDesc: Review recommended. Real security exposure detected but no evidence of actual harm yet.
riskTooltip.lowDesc: Periodic review. Not a security event on its own, but may signal a larger issue.

audit.sensitivePathsDesc: LOW/MEDIUM — Flags file access to sensitive locations. Key files (.env, SSH keys, keychains) trigger MEDIUM. Directory and generic matches (.ssh/, *.pem, *token*) trigger LOW. If a secret is found in the output, Sensitive Data also triggers.
audit.sensitiveDataDesc: MEDIUM — Regex patterns detect credentials (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys, PII) in tool output.
audit.dataExfiltrationDesc: HIGH — Detects when an agent sends local file content or exposed credentials to external destinations.

exfilCmds.curlUpload: Upload local file via curl form data
exfilCmds.curlDataBinary: Upload local file via curl binary data
exfilCmds.pipeToNetwork: Pipe file content to a network tool
exfilCmds.scpOut: Copy local file to a remote host

audit.exfilCredentialEscalationDesc: When a credential (API key, token, password, private key) is detected in tool output, the system checks all subsequent external calls (web_fetch, web_search) in the same session. If any of those calls contain a fragment of the exposed credential in their URL or request body, the event is escalated to HIGH risk — the secret has left your environment.
audit.dangerousCommandsDesc: HIGH — Destructive commands (rm -rf, curl|bash, mkfs) trigger HIGH. Elevated commands (sudo, ssh, curl, cat .env) trigger MEDIUM.
audit.promptInjectionDesc: HIGH — Detects active injection attacks in user input: instruction override, role hijacking, data exfiltration requests, jailbreak attempts.
audit.anomalyDetectionDesc: LOW/MEDIUM — Unusual hours and unusual call volume trigger LOW. Unknown domains (never seen in 30-day baseline) trigger MEDIUM.
audit.rescanDesc: Custom rules only apply to new sessions by default. Click "Rescan" to rescan all existing sessions with your current rules.
audit.noCredentials: No credentials detected in any audit events.
audit.infoFileRead: Agent read a file (Read, Image tools). Flagged when reading sensitive paths (.ssh, .env, keychains) or when output contains secrets.
audit.infoFileWrite: Agent wrote or edited a file (Write, Edit tools). Flagged when targeting sensitive paths or system config files.
audit.infoExec: Agent executed a shell command (Bash, Exec tools). Flagged for destructive commands (rm -rf, chmod 777), elevated commands (sudo, ssh, curl), or data exfiltration patterns.
audit.infoWebFetch: Agent made an HTTP request (WebFetch tool). Flagged when accessing domains not seen in the agent's baseline history.
audit.infoWebSearch: Agent performed a web search (WebSearch tool). Flagged when the search domain is unknown to the agent's baseline.
audit.riskHighAction: Immediate action required. Damage has occurred or is in progress.
audit.riskMediumAction: Review recommended. Real security exposure detected but no evidence of actual harm yet.
audit.riskLowAction: Periodic review. Not a security event on its own, but may signal a larger issue.
audit.descDataLeak: Data Leak — A command uploaded local file content to an external URL (e.g. curl -F file=@/path https://...). Data has already been sent.
audit.descDestructiveCmd: Destructive Command — Agent executed a destructive command: rm -rf, curl|bash, chmod 777/+s, mkfs, dd of=/dev/, iptables -F.
audit.descPromptInjection: Prompt Injection — Active injection attack detected in user input: instruction override, role hijacking, data exfiltration requests, jailbreak attempts.
audit.descCredentialExfil: Credential Exfiltrated — A secret (API key, token, password) appeared in tool output, and was then confirmed sent in a subsequent external network call. The secret has left your environment.
audit.descCredentialExposed: Credential Exposed — API key, token, password, or private key appeared in tool output. The secret is in the agent's memory but not confirmed sent externally.
audit.descElevatedCmd: Elevated Command — High-privilege command executed: sudo, ssh, scp, curl, cat .env.
audit.descUnknownDomain: Unknown Domain — Agent accessed a domain never seen in its baseline history.
audit.descSensitivePath: Sensitive Path — Agent read or wrote a sensitive path (.env, .ssh/, keychains), but no secret was found in the output. If a secret is found, it escalates to Medium.
audit.descUnusualHours: Unusual Hours — Activity outside the agent's typical active hours (compared against 30-day baseline).
audit.descUnusualVolume: Unusual Volume — Session tool call count exceeds 3x the agent's historical average.

security.toolsUsedDesc: What tools this agent calls, ranked by frequency.
security.fileAccessDesc: Directories this agent has read or written files in, ranked by access count.
security.externalDesc: External domains this agent has contacted via web_fetch or web_search.
security.localOnly: Local only — this agent has not made any external network calls.

recPatterns.rotateCredentials: Rotate the affected credentials and review file access.
recPatterns.addWhitelist: Add a whitelist rule in Audit Rules → Sensitive Paths, or review if expected.
recPatterns.reviewDomains: Review these domains. They will be added to baseline automatically over time.
recPatterns.ranElevatedCommands: Ran {{count}} elevated commands (sudo, ssh, curl, etc.)
recPatterns.reviewCommands: Review if these commands are necessary.
recPatterns.rotateCredentialsExfil: Rotate the affected credentials and review why the agent is reading these files.

security.verdictTooltipIntro: Agent-level risk assessment based on active findings from audit scans:

verdictTooltip.caution: Has medium or low severity findings that need review
verdictTooltip.unsafe: Has prompt injection attacks or high severity credential exposures

security.trustTooltipIntro: Follows Zero Trust Network principles — never trust, always verify. This level reflects how predictable and contained the agent's external network behavior is:

trustTooltip.local: Agent has made no external network calls — fully contained
trustTooltip.transparent: All external calls target known, baselined domains — behavior is predictable
trustTooltip.opaque: Agent accessed unknown domains or exhibited data exfiltration patterns — requires investigation

timeline.exfilDesc: The sensitive data above ({{type}}) was read by the agent, and then sent out in a subsequent external network call. The following calls were made after the data was read:

flagDescs.exfil_pattern: Command uploads local file content to an external URL
flagDescs.new_domain_generic: Domain not seen in baseline history
flagDescs.anomaly_hour: This event at {{hour}}:00 is outside this agent's typical hours ({{ranges}})
flagDescs.anomaly_hour_generic: Activity outside normal hours for this agent
flagDescs.anomaly_volume: Session tool call count exceeds 3x the agent's average ({{avg}} calls/session)
flagDescs.anomaly_volume_generic: Unusually high tool call volume in this session
flagDescs.anomaly_path_detail: Path not in agent's typical directories ({{paths}})
flagDescs.anomaly_path_generic: Path not in agent's typical directories
flagDescs.sensitive_data: Secret detected in tool output — see Sensitive Data Matches below
flagDescs.prompt_injection: Injection pattern detected — see details below

timeline.failedExportCSV: Failed to export CSV. Check your network connection.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: agent context files · workspace-{{agent}}/
memory.noFiles: No memory files for this agent. Files are discovered from the agent's workspace directory.
memory.name: Name
memory.tokenTooltip1: This is an estimated token count for the file, calculated by dividing the character count by 4 — a standard approximation for English and code text. Non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese) may cost more tokens per character, so treat this as a rough lower bound.
memory.tokenTooltip2: Memory files are automatically injected into the system prompt at the start of every session. Their token cost is therefore a per-session fixed overhead — the larger the file, the more context window space it consumes before any conversation begins.

live.title: Live Monitor
live.allQuiet: All quiet — no agents currently active
live.autoUpdate: This page updates automatically when an agent starts working.
live.legendStuck: Stuck — no updates for 5–30 min with failures
live.legendIdle: Idle — no activity in last 30 min, shown for 3h
live.ingestDelay: Data may appear with a slight delay due to the time it takes OpenClaw to flush activity to disk.

cron.title: Cron
cron.subtitle: {{total}} jobs · {{enabled}} enabled
cron.runsTooltipDataSourceBody: Run records (timestamp, status, duration, model, tokens) are stored in OpenClaw's own run log:
cron.runsTooltipSessionRefBody: Each run may reference a full conversation transcript. When it exists, a "See in Sessions →" link appears. The two relevant files are:
cron.runsTooltipPrune: Why some runs have no session link
cron.runsTooltipPruneBody: OpenClaw prunes cron run sessions after 24 hours by default. Once the session JSONL is deleted, the link disappears — but the run record itself remains.

sessionTimeline.contentNotAvailable: Message content not available — session JSONL file may have been removed.
sessionTimeline.searchPlaceholder: Search by agent or paste session ID…
sessionTimeline.noTextContent: No text content in this message.
sessionTimeline.pageSubtitle: Session execution Gantt — LLM inference & tool calls
sessionTimeline.idleGapsDesc: A period where no events occur — no LLM calls, no tool execution, no tool results. This typically means the user stepped away (phone call, break, overnight pause).
sessionTimeline.howItWorksDesc: The timeline tracks timestamps from messages, tool call starts, and tool call completions. Only gaps where none of these events occur for longer than 5 minutes are compressed.
sessionTimeline.notCompressedDesc: Long-running tools (e.g. a 10-minute exec) are preserved at real duration — their start and end timestamps prevent the gap from being flagged as idle.
sessionTimeline.wallDurationTooltip:
Total real clock time from the session's first event to its last — including all idle gaps.

Like a stopwatch that runs the entire time, even when nothing is happening.
sessionTimeline.activeTimeTooltip:
Wall time minus all idle gaps longer than 30 s.

This is the time the agent was actually doing work: LLM inference + tool execution. Shown as the Gantt chart's time axis.
sessionTimeline.llmApiCallsTooltip:
Number of LLM API calls made in this session. Each row in the Gantt = one API call.

Every tool call originates from an LLM API call — the model responds and requests one or more tools (e.g. Bash, Edit, Read). After the tools run, the agent makes another LLM API call with the results.

Not all LLM API calls trigger tools — some just produce a text reply and finish with stop reason 'stop'.
sessionTimeline.toolCallsTooltip:
Total number of tool invocations across all LLM API calls.

Includes Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Agent, and others. One LLM API call can trigger multiple tool calls in a single response.
sessionTimeline.llmTimeTooltip:
Time the model spent generating a response — from receiving the request to returning the first token.
Does not include tool execution.
sessionTimeline.toolTimeTooltip:
Total wall-clock time spent executing tools across all turns.

Parallel-aware: when multiple tools run simultaneously within a turn, only the wall-clock span is counted (not the sum of individual durations).
sessionTimeline.stopReasonTooltipTitle: Stop reason — why this LLM API call ended
sessionTimeline.stopReasonStop: Model finished naturally. No further action needed — this is the normal "done" state. Oftentimes, a turn with stop reason "stop" will have an Assistant Response with text content.
sessionTimeline.stopReasonToolUse: Model decided to call a tool. The agent runs the tool locally and feeds the result back into the next LLM call.
sessionTimeline.stopReasonLength: Hit the max_tokens limit and the response was cut off mid-generation.
sessionTimeline.stopReasonError: Something went wrong — network failure, API error, or an unexpected provider response.
sessionTimeline.stopReasonAborted: The user manually cancelled the request before it completed.
sessionTimeline.noTimingData: No timing data available for this session.
sessionTimeline.noDataForSession: No data found for this session.

debugReplay.title: Cache Trace
debugReplay.subtitle: Per-turn pipeline replay — each turn shows Steps (model calls) within the agentic loop
debugReplay.searchPlaceholder: Search by model or paste session ID…
debugReplay.loadedDesc: Raw conversation history loaded from the session file on disk.
debugReplay.sanitizedDesc: Cleanup pass — removes invalid tool calls, fixes malformed content, strips empty messages.
debugReplay.limitedDesc: Context-window enforcement — if messages exceed the model limit, the oldest are dropped to fit.
debugReplay.promptDesc: System prompt and user instructions assembled, ready to prepend to the conversation.
debugReplay.imagesDesc: Image attachments processed (resized / converted). The note shows how many images are included.
debugReplay.contextDesc: Final message array sent to the model API for this call.
debugReplay.afterDesc: Session state after the model responds — the assistant reply (and any tool results) are now appended.
debugReplay.cacheTraceNote: Note: This tool requires a cache trace.
debugReplay.letOpenClawEnable: 2. Let OpenClaw enable it for you
debugReplay.copyFlag: Copy this flag to OpenClaw and let it enable for you.
debugReplay.fileGrowsWarning: This file grows quickly — set up a cron job to clean it periodically.
debugReplay.pipelineDesc: each stage transforms the context before it reaches the model. Steps = model calls within the agentic loop.
debugReplay.turnUsageSubtitle: total tokens and cost across {{count}} model call{{plural}} in this turn.
debugReplay.modelConfigSubtitle: model parameters and cost rates used for this turn.
debugReplay.roleDistributionSubtitle: breakdown of message roles sent to the model.
debugReplay.userPromptSubtitle: the assembled prompt OpenClaw constructed from the incoming request.
debugReplay.contextTailSubtitle: last {{count}} messages the model receives. The most recent context before the first call.
debugReplay.hasTokenUsageDesc: = this assistant message carries token billing data (input/output/cache tokens and cost) from its model call.
debugReplay.noCacheTraceData: No cache-trace data for this session.
debugReplay.systemPromptHashTooltip: Hash of the system prompt. If this matches across turns, the system prompt did not change.
debugReplay.messagesHashTooltip: The hash of all messages changed — content or metadata was modified, even if count stayed the same.

debugContext.title: Context Breakdown
debugContext.subtitle: Per-step context composition — System, History, and Tool tokens across each API call
debugContext.searchPlaceholder: Search by model or paste session ID…
debugContext.cacheTraceNote: Note: This tool requires a cache trace.
debugContext.letOpenClawEnable: 2. Let OpenClaw enable it for you
debugContext.copyFlag: Copy this flag to OpenClaw and let it enable for you.
debugContext.fileGrowsWarning: This file grows quickly — set up a cron job to clean it periodically.
debugContext.cacheTraceNotFound: Cache Trace Log Data Not Found
debugContext.systemDesc: System prompt — CLAUDE.md, tool schemas, memory files. Mostly fixed per session.
debugContext.historyDesc: Conversation history — all prior user + assistant messages. Grows with every step.
debugContext.toolsDesc: Tool results — Bash output, file reads, grep output. Most volatile; one large read can spike this.
debugContext.systemTooltip:
Estimated tokens for the system prompt.

Includes CLAUDE.md, tool schemas, memory files.
Mostly fixed per session.

Calculated as char count ÷ 4 (approximation)
debugContext.historyTooltip:
Estimated tokens for conversation history.

All prior user + assistant messages.
Grows linearly with every step.

Calculated as char count ÷ 4 (approximation)
debugContext.toolsTooltip:
Estimated tokens for tool results.

Bash output, file reads, grep results.
Most volatile — one large file read can spike this significantly.

Calculated as char count ÷ 4 (approximation)
debugContext.totalTokenTooltip:
Total prompt tokens for this step:
  input (non-cached) + cache_read + cache_write

This is the actual context size fed to the model, from the DB usage record.

More accurate than the System+History+Tools estimates above, which use char ÷ 4 approximation.
debugContext.pressureTooltipPrefix:
How much of the {{capacity}} context window is consumed this step.

Context Pressure = Total Token Used ÷ {{capacityNum}}

deepTurns.title: Deep Turns
deepTurns.flagsDesc: Flags agent turns where the LLM needed ≥
deepTurns.deepTurnExplain: A deep turn is any user-turn where the agent made ≥ {{threshold}} LLM calls before completing.
deepTurns.uniqueRatioExplain: Unique tool+args combinations / total calls. A low ratio (< 30%) means the agent repeatedly invoked the same tool with the same inputs — likely stuck.
deepTurns.normalizedExplain: Tool arguments are normalized before comparison: timestamps, UUIDs, absolute paths (→ basename), and large numbers (> 10¹²) are stripped so that semantically identical calls produce the same key.
deepTurns.noDeepTurns: No deep turns match the current filters.
deepTurns.noDeepTurnsAll: No deep turns detected. All agent turns completed in fewer than

profiler.title: Profiler
profiler.subtitle: Tool execution & response latency profiling
profiler.searchPlaceholder: Search by agent or paste session ID…

tokenUsage.title: Token Usage
tokenUsage.noTokenData: No token usage recorded yet. Data will appear after the first session.
tokenUsage.noTrendData: No trend data for this range.
tokenUsage.priceNote: : pricing varies by model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)
tokenUsage.tokenTotalNote: not a billing metric — just a composition of all token types
tokenUsage.costFormula: ≈ Input × rate + Output × rate + CacheRead × rate. Output is typically the most expensive per token; CacheRead rate is very low (often ~10% of Input rate), so cached tokens save significantly on cost.
tokenUsage.costNote1: P_output is typically the highest-priced tier
tokenUsage.costNote2: P_read is significantly discounted — often around 10% of P_input (standard input rate)
tokenUsage.costNote3: cache_write may be billed separately depending on the provider, though some platforms incorporate it into the standard input pricing
tokenUsage.costDisclaimer: Cost figures are estimated based on OpenClaw's built-in pricing logic. For authoritative billing, refer to your model provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.).
